name: Peoples Bank Of Graceville
description_short: Community bank in Graceville, FL offering FDIC-insured deposit accounts, consumer and commercial loans, and digital banking services with local relationship-focused lending.
description_long:
Peoples Bank of Graceville is an independent community bank headquartered in Graceville, Florida, positioning itself as a hometown banking alternative with emphasis on personal service and local decision-making. The bank markets itself as a relationship-driven institution where loans are approved by local lenders rather than distant corporate entities, differentiating from larger regional and national banks.

The bank offers a full suite of deposit products including checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, and IRAs. On the lending side, they provide consumer loans, commercial loans, real estate loans, construction loans, farm loans, and mortgage loans. Digital banking services include PBG Online internet banking, PBOG Mobile app with mobile deposit capability, CheckFree bill pay, Zelle money transfer, telephone banking, and eStatements. Additional services include merchant services, night depository, safe deposit boxes, Visa check cards, and ATM access.

What distinguishes Peoples Bank of Graceville is its explicit commitment to community banking and local underwriting. The marketing emphasizes small-town values, known and trusted customer service representatives, and the convenience of banking with lenders who understand the local community. They highlight FDIC insurance backing as a security feature and position their digital services as modern conveniences layered onto traditional relationship banking.

The bank appears well-established with standard banking operations and reasonable digital capabilities for a community institution. However, as a small local bank, they likely have limited product customization, potentially higher fees than large national competitors, and geographic restriction to their local market. The website indicates upcoming digital service improvements, suggesting current digital offerings may be basic compared to fintech competitors. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: PBG Online internet banking with bill pay
PBOG Mobile app with mobile deposit
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Local loan approval process with community-based lenders rather than automated systems
Mobile deposit capability through PBOG Mobile app for remote check deposits
Multiple digital banking channels: internet banking, mobile app, and telephone banking available 24/7
Zelle money transfer integration for peer-to-peer payments
Real-time account alerts and eStatements with check images accessible online
Wide range of loan products including farm loans and construction loans less common at larger banks
cons: Limited to Graceville, FL location with single branch, restricting accessibility for non-local customers
No mention of competitive interest rates or fee structures compared to larger competitors
Website indicates digital services are undergoing changes, suggesting current platforms may be outdated or limited
No information provided about minimum account balances, monthly fees, or overdraft policies
Limited business hours (Thursday closes at noon, Saturday drive-thru only until noon) compared to larger banks
best_for: Graceville, FL and surrounding Jackson County residents seeking relationship-based community banking
Small business owners and farmers needing local agricultural and commercial lending decisions
Customers prioritizing personal service and local decision-making over large national bank impersonality
Depositors seeking FDIC-insured accounts with basic-to-intermediate digital banking capabilities
diagnosis: Peoples Bank of Graceville is best for Graceville-area residents and small business owners who value relationship banking and local lending decisions over convenience and digital innovation. The main caveat is geographic limitation to a single small-town location and lack of information about competitive pricing—customers should verify fees and rates directly before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
